title: Claude Dev Team Gate
description: Base contract for every pipeline gate JSON written under pipeline/gates/. Per-stage schemas extend this with stage-specific required fields. Field semantics are defined in .claude/rules/gates.md; the gate-validator hook is the authoritative reader.

stage.description: Stage identifier matching the gate's filename, e.g. 'stage-04' or 'stage-05-backend'.

status.description: Outcome of the stage. ESCALATE halts the pipeline regardless of any newer gate; see .claude/rules/escalation.md.

agent.description: Name of the agent (or 'orchestrator') that wrote this gate. Used for ownership and retry routing.

track.description: Pipeline track the gate belongs to. Tracks differ on which stages run and how many approvals each gate requires.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the gate was written. Used for the bypassed-escalation sweep.

blockers.description: Reasons the stage cannot proceed. A non-empty array typically pairs with status FAIL or ESCALATE.

warnings.description: Non-blocking concerns surfaced for visibility. Do not change the gate status.

retry_number.description: Zero-based retry counter. When >= 1, this_attempt_differs_by must be a non-empty string (enforced by gate-validator).

previous_failure_reason.description: Short summary of why the previous attempt failed; informational only.

this_attempt_differs_by.description: What changed since the previous failure. Required when retry_number >= 1; same content twice escalates instead of retrying.
